name: La Hoz Somera and Los Castillejos - circular from Villanueva de Alcorón via Valsalobre
name_original: La Hoz Somera y Los Castillejos – circular desde Villanueva de Alcorón por Valsalobre
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villanueva De Alcorón offers access to diverse terrain within and near the Alto Tajo Natural Park. This region is characterized by its expansive forests, deep valleys, and varied landscapes, providing a range of mountain bike trails. The area features routes that traverse both paved and unpaved segments, with elevations suitable for different fitness levels. Cyclists can explore the natural beauty of the park, which is known for its extensive natural environment. Best mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Alcorón The most popular mountain bike trail is La Hoz Somera and Los Castillejos - circular from Villanueva de Alcorón via Valsalobre, a 30.8 miles (49.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Hundido de Armallones – circular desde Arbeteta por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a moderate 31.2 miles (50.2 km) path. This route explores the natural features of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Local mountain bikers also love the Laguna de Taravilla – Trail along the Tajo River loop from Peñalén, a 45.2 miles (72.7 km) trail leading through river valleys and natural areas, often completed in about 6 hours 29 minutes. Mountain biking around Villanueva De Alcorón is defined by its valleys, forests, and the extensive Alto Tajo Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult mountain bike terrain. The routes in Villanueva De Alcorón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 5 reviews. More than 80 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Alcorón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Alcorón offer diverse terrain, ranging from paved sections to unpaved segments within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You'll encounter expansive forests, deep valleys, and varied landscapes, with some routes requiring good fitness and potentially involving pushing your bike on more challenging unpaved sections.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, the area caters to various skill levels. While there are no easy trails listed, you'll find 4 moderate routes and 3 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both intermediate riders and those seeking a significant challenge can find suitable options.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking?
A: The trails often traverse the scenic Alto Tajo Natural Park, known for its valleys and forests. You can encounter impressive natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5331775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Peñalén&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5373777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4442967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sima de Alcorón Cave&lt;/a&gt;. Another highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo River Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt; within the park.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, you can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004930583/la-hoz-somera-and-los-castillejos-circular-from-villanueva-de-alcoron-via-valsalobre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hoz Somera and Los Castillejos - circular from Villanueva de Alcorón via Valsalobre&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 49.6 km journey. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004696261/hundido-de-armallones-circular-desde-arbeteta-por-el-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundido de Armallones – circular desde Arbeteta por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, a 50.2 km moderate route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: Given its location within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, the region offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but the forests provide shade. Winter might be suitable for some, but always check local weather conditions as some higher elevation areas could experience colder temperatures.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Alcorón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 5 reviews. Many riders appreciate the diverse terrain and the opportunity to explore the extensive natural environment of the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking longer distances and more challenging terrain, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14887130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Taravilla – Trail along the Tajo River loop from Peñalén&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 72.7 km trail. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River – Suspension bridge over the Tajo River loop from Zaorejas&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 92.9 km.

Q: Is it possible to rent mountain bikes in the Alto Tajo Natural Park area?
A: Yes, bike rentals are available within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, making it convenient for visitors who do not bring their own equipment. This allows for greater accessibility to the diverse mountain biking opportunities in the region.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: There are 7 mountain bike trails documented around Villanueva De Alcorón, offering a range of experiences within and near the Alto Tajo Natural Park. These include 4 moderate and 3 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any places to explore or points of interest near the trails?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, you can explore the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5166717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valsalobre&lt;/a&gt;, which is often part of circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4040502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vado Salmeron Shelter&lt;/a&gt; also provides a point of interest along some routes. The entire Alto Tajo Natural Park is rich in natural beauty and offers many opportunities for exploration.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on mountain bike routes in this region?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the route's difficulty and length. For moderate trails, you might expect around 500-900 meters of ascent, while more difficult and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River – Suspension bridge over the Tajo River loop from Zaorejas&lt;/a&gt;, can feature over 1300 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find shorter mountain bike routes for a quicker ride?
A: While many routes are longer, a moderate option like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcantud loop from Herrería de Santa Cristina&lt;/a&gt; offers a shorter ride at approximately 29.9 km, which can typically be completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes.
tips:
It's a small town with few inhabitants and little social life, and an elderly population.
It has running water, like most towns in the area, and a church in reasonable condition. It's an ideal place to escape the hustle and bustle of the world for a few hours. The only bar was closed the day I visited.

PR 01 Path through La Pedriza. It is a route for people very used to mountain terrain and in good physical shape. It is a tour that must be done in summer, since it takes a whole day to complete the itinerary.
